title: Task-Spec Frontmatter v2.2
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter block at the top of a Task-Spec v2.2 file. Mirrors the field reference in references/concepts/agent-contract.md. v2.2 makes the sign-off envelope (signed_off_sig) a real, key-optional HMAC-SHA256 contract.

id.description: Task identifier. Must match T-YYYYMMDD-slug. Must equal filename basename.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Only ready specs are eligible for dispatch.

format_version.description: Task-Spec format version. v2 is current; v1 retained for legacy.

agent.description: Preferred agent (any, claude, codex, kimi, cursor, etc.).

depends_on.description: List of T-IDs that must be done before this task is ready.

touches_paths.description: Authoritative list of paths this task is allowed to modify.

source_note.description: Free-form note about where this task originated.

created.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the spec was authored.

execution_backend.description: Target executor type. Used by dispatch tooling to route the task.

signed_off.description: Autonomy contract flag. ONLY safe-to-delegate.sh --stamp may set this to true.

signed_off_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the stamp, or (none).

task_spec_version.description: Version of the task-spec skill that authored the file.

signed_off_sig.description: B2 HMAC sign-off envelope (v2.2). Format: hmac-sha256-v1:<keyid>:<hex>. The HMAC-SHA256 is computed by safe-to-delegate.sh --stamp over a CANONICAL fixed-field payload: newline-joined id, body_digest (sha256 of the spec body after the closing frontmatter '---'), signed_off, signed_off_by, signed_off_at — EXCLUDING this signed_off_sig line itself and independent of frontmatter line order. keyid is the first 8 hex of sha256(key). KEY-OPTIONAL: this field is present only when a signing key was resolved at stamp time (TASKSPEC_SIGNING_KEY or .git/info/taskspec-signing-key) AND a sha256 provider (openssl|shasum|sha256sum) was available. When absent, the sign-off is structural-only (Tier 2). validate-task-spec.sh Check 17 recomputes and compares: match = Tier 1 (full crypto trust); absent/no-key = Tier 2 (structural-only, supervised dispatch only); mismatch/malformed = Tier 3 (hard fail). HMAC is symmetric: it binds 'a repo-key holder stamped this', not per-author non-repudiation.

signed_off_envelope.description: DEPRECATED structured form of the sign-off envelope, retained for forward-compatibility. The realized v2.2 envelope is the flat string field signed_off_sig (hmac-sha256-v1:<keyid>:<hex>) written by safe-to-delegate.sh --stamp; that string is the source of truth. This object form is reserved for a future per-author detached-signature upgrade (e.g. Ed25519/DSSE) and is not written by the current gate.

signature.description: Hex MAC over the canonical payload.
